question_title: Will a Gallup Korea Daily Opinion poll released before July 10, 2026 show President Lee Jae-myung's disapproval rating equal to or above his approval rating?
summary: Threshold question: will Gallup's approve-disapprove gap (currently +10 as of Jun26) cross to ≤0 in the Jul3 or Jul10 report. Pure momentum extrapolation (-6/week) implies ~50-62%, but two pieces of counter-evidence pull it down: (1) Realmeter, the leading indicator Gallup lags, has flattened at the dead cross (only -0.2pp this week), signaling the underlying decline is decelerating rather than continuing in freefall; (2) Gallup carries a persistent ~+10-13 house-effect buffer over Realmeter, so its equilibrium likely sits above zero. A Monte Carlo scenario mixture (strong catch-up decline / moderate / stabilize, with release uncertainty) yields ~0.32. I nudge up to 0.38 because Gallup's sharp drop is more recent than Realmeter's, so it may still have 1-2 weeks of decline before flattening. Net: moderately below even, NO favored but with real crossing probability.

factors.description: Strong recent Gallup momentum: gap fell +22 (Jun12) to +10 (Jun26); only ~10 more points needed with two chances (Jul3, Jul10)
Realmeter (leading indicator Gallup typically lags) already at sustained dead cross, suggesting Gallup is directionally heading there
Realmeter decline has flattened (-0.2pp this week) at the cross, signaling underlying stabilization rather than continued freefall
Gallup runs structurally ~+10-13 more favorable to Lee on the gap than Realmeter; even matching Realmeter leaves Gallup well above zero
Release/schedule risk: Gallup numbering (665->666 over two weeks) hints at irregular cadence; a July 3 report may not appear, reducing chances

reasoning:
**Forecast: 38% (NO favored)**

This is a threshold question — whether Gallup's approve-disapprove gap (currently +10 as of June 26) crosses to ≤0 in the July 3 or July 10 report.

**Key reasoning:**
- **Pushing YES:** Sharp recent Gallup momentum (gap fell from +22 to +10 in two weeks), Realmeter already at a sustained dead cross, and two chances to cross.
- **Pushing NO (decisive):** Realmeter — the leading indicator Gallup typically lags — has *flattened* at -0.2pp this week, signaling the underlying decline is decelerating, not in freefall. And Gallup carries a persistent ~+10-13 house-effect buffer over Realmeter, so even fully "catching up" leaves Gallup's gap above zero.

A Monte Carlo scenario mixture yielded ~0.32; I nudged up to 0.38 because Gallup's drop is more recent than Realmeter's and may have a week or two of decline left. The premortem reviewer approved, placing its independent range at 0.32-0.42.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if any Gallup Korea Daily Opinion (데일리 오피니언) report released between June 27, 2026 and July 10, 2026 (inclusive) reports President Lee Jae-myung's presidential job-performance disapproval rating ("잘못하고 있다") as equal to or greater than his approval rating ("잘하고 있다"). The relevant figures are the headline weekly percentages in the report. Reports are published at https://www.gallup.co.kr/gallupdb/report.asp (look for the most recent "데일리 오피니언 제○○○호" entries). It resolves NO if every such report released in this window shows approval strictly greater than disapproval.
fine_print: Only Gallup Korea's own self-commissioned Daily Opinion presidential job-evaluation figures count; other pollsters (e.g., Realmeter, NBS) and special/commissioned Gallup surveys do not. If multiple qualifying reports are released in the window, a YES in any one of them resolves the question YES. "Released between June 27, 2026 and July 10, 2026" refers to the report's publication date as shown on the Gallup site (typically Fridays at 10:00 KST). If Gallup does not release any Daily Opinion presidential job-evaluation report in this window (for example due to an early summer break), the question resolves NO. If the approval and disapproval figures are reported as exactly equal, the question resolves YES. If the Gallup site is unavailable, the figures as reported by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) citing the Gallup Daily Opinion report will be used.

factors.description: Strong negative momentum: Gallup gap fell from +22 (Jun12) to +10 (Jun26), -6/week approval decline; needs only ~10 more points to dead cross with two chances (Jul3, Jul10)
Realmeter (leading indicator) already at sustained dead cross; Gallup historically lags it, suggesting Gallup is heading there
Realmeter decline has decelerated sharply (only -0.2pp this week after flattening at dead cross), signaling underlying stabilization rather than continued freefall
Gallup runs structurally ~+10-13 more favorable to Lee on the gap than Realmeter; matching Realmeter's level still leaves Gallup well above zero
Release/schedule risk: Gallup numbering (665->666 over two weeks) hints at irregular schedule; July 3 report may not appear, reducing to one chance

input.anchor: Random-walk/momentum extrapolation of the gap: ~0.50 base before deceleration evidence
input.assessment: Threshold question on the approve-disapprove gap crossing zero in Gallup. Current gap +10 (Jun26). Monte Carlo mixture of drift scenarios (strong catch-up decline 35%, moderate 40%, stabilize 25%) with two report chances yields ~0.32; momentum-only models give 0.50-0.62. Key tension: strong recent Gallup momentum and Realmeter-as-leading-indicator (up) vs. Realmeter's decline flattening at the cross and Gallup's structural favorability (down). Gallup's drop is more recent so it may still have 1-2 weeks of decline left, nudging me above the pure mixture to ~0.38.
input.key_uncertainties: Whether Gallup's decline continues at full momentum or decelerates like Realmeter; whether both July reports release.
input.tool_audit: research() returned rich, current data (Realmeter Jun29 flattening figure was decisive). execute_code Monte Carlo worked cleanly for scenario mixing. No tool failures.
input.process_reflection: Smooth pipeline. The research sub-agent surfaced the critical Realmeter deceleration datapoint that flipped my prior from ~0.50 down to ~0.38. For threshold-on-a-trend questions, a small tool to fit drift+noise from a short polling time series would reduce manual Monte Carlo guesswork.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
Verified the forecast against the research trace and worldview store. The forecast is well-constructed and grounded.

FACTOR GROUNDING:
- Factor 1 (Gallup +22→+10, -6/week): Confirmed in trace — June 12 = 57-35 = +22, June 26 = 51-41 = +10. Grounded.
- Factor 2 (Realmeter dead cross, Gallup lags): Research confirms Realmeter sustained dead cross 2 weeks (46.5/49.5). Sign +0.7 (YES) matches description.
- Factor 3 (Realmeter decelerated -0.2pp = stabilization): Confirmed. Sign -0.8 (NO) correct.
- Factor 4 (Gallup ~+13 more favorable than Realmeter): Gallup gap +10 vs Realmeter -3 = ~13pp difference. Grounded. Sign -0.9 (NO) correct.
- Factor 5 (665→666 numbering / schedule risk): This specific claim is NOT visible in the provided trace excerpt. The tool metrics show fetch_url and web_search calls (1437ms, 15496ms) that don't appear in the excerpt — the Gallup report-number data likely came from there. This is a low-weight factor (logit -0.3, conf 0.4), not load-bearing, so at most a trivial concern.

No hallucination (core claims grounded), no double-counting (factors 2 and 3/4 draw on Realmeter but cut in opposite directions and are distinct aspects), no wrong-direction factors.

RESOLUTION CRITERIA: Provided (not MISSING). The agent engaged correctly with the specific terms: it tracks Gallup's own Daily Opinion figures only, correctly excludes Realmeter as merely a leading indicator (per fine print), handles the "equal counts as YES" rule (gap ≤0), and models the two release chances (July 3, July 10) plus release/schedule risk. No misalignment.

PRE-PUBLICATION EVENT: None. The June 26 Gallup report (+10 gap, NO) predates the June 27 window start. No factor treats the question as pre-resolved. Published June 29 is consistent. Fine.

PROBABILITY: My independent estimate aligns with the agent's. Gallup must cross from +10 to ≤0 within 1-2 readings. The recent -12 single-cycle swing supports a bull case, but Realmeter (leading indicator) has flattened at the cross and Gallup's ~13pp structural buffer implies an equilibrium well above zero. The Monte Carlo mixture (~0.32) plus a modest recency-of-decline nudge to 0.38 is a defensible synthesis. I'd put the range at 0.32–0.42; 0.38 sits sensibly within it. No meaningful divergence.

SELF-EXAMINATION: Counterargument is strong, not a token gesture — it builds a coherent alternative story (house-effect equilibrium ~+7-10, single reading was catch-up not freefall, no fresh catalyst for another collapse). Confidence 0.60 is well-calibrated given the agent's own surfaced drift-sensitivity (27%–62%). Anchor (0.50) to final (0.38) is a ~12pp departure justified by well-sourced deceleration/structural-favorability evidence, below the 20pp concern threshold.

TOOL AUDIT: Narrative "No tool failures" matches ground truth (0 errors). No discrepancy.

WORLDVIEW CONSISTENCY: Three related research entries (all fresh, no numeric sub-forecast on this quantity) corroborate the factors — Realmeter 46.5/49.5, Gallup 51/41, active drivers (NEC scandal, economy, base erosion). No contradictions.

Only trivial issue: Factor 5's numbering detail isn't in the visible trace excerpt, but it's non-load-bearing. Approve.
